core_mechanism: a density-driven collective instability (nonequilibrium dynamical phase transition) in a system of locally interacting self-driven agents, in which a homogeneous flowing state becomes linearly unstable above a critical density and spontaneously organizes into clustered/blocked structures
brief_reason: The abstract describes self-driven many-particle systems that, above certain densities, spontaneously form 'phantom' jams, stop-and-go waves, and lanes with sharp threshold-like onset and universal features, which is the signature of a critical-density collective instability driving self-organized pattern formation (with a micro-to-macro coarse-graining hierarchy linking particle-based to fluid-dynamic descriptions).
